mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the market reads lower policy discount rates and easier financial conditions, which should lift S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not precede positive 20-day excess returns.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of instances when FEDFUNDS declines over the prior month, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for 2 years, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation break above the recent trend implies sticky inflation and higher real-rate pressure, which should compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average by more than 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations fail to show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign accuracy falls below 40% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE combined with slower PAYEMS growth signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income momentum, which should reduce cyclical earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising UNRATE and decelerating PAYEMS does not predict lower 60-day forward returns versus unconditional returns, or if the interaction term is not negative in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates, it signals stronger retail demand and better near-term revenue growth for large-cap equities, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive after at least 2 consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: Reject if retail sales rebounds are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if rank correlation remains below 0.03 across rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO indicates improving industrial activity and better earnings breadth for cyclicals, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through a near-term growth surprise channel.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change is positive and above its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO changes do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the effect disappears in any 18-month rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in HOUST and RSAFS marks broad demand expansion across housing and consumer spending, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is strongest when both series improve together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in HOUST and RSAFS fails to outperform single-series signals in predicting 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not positive in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle slowdown, a rising UNRATE should be more equity-negative when PAYEMS also weakens, because labor slack then reflects genuine recession risk rather than benign rebalancing. This should forecast negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment shocks without PAYEMS deterioration do not underperform the joint condition, or if the joint signal does not produce negative median 20-day SPX returns in triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is still high, the market interprets policy as restrictive and sticky inflation as a valuation headwind, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if easing policy offsets the inflation pressure.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-rate episodes are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined condition yields positive returns in more than 40% of cases.
